tossups.question: A recording of this jazz standard begins with Sam Jones playing the bass ostinato “quarter note G, straight eighths B-flat – D, quarter notes E – D” before Miles Davis plays the melody in a rare appearance as sideman. A 1956 film with the same title was inspired by a recording of this standard on Nat King Cole Sings for Two in Love. This standard’s A section consists of a circle of fifths progression beginning on the minor four. Although the Real Book renders it in (*) E minor, this standard begins with the notes “G, A, B-flat, long E-flat” in the more common key of G minor, as on Cannonball Adderley’s Somethin’ Else. Joseph Kosma and Jacques Prévert wrote this originally French-language standard. For 10 points, name this standard about “red and gold” objects that “drift by the window” during the title season.
tossups.question_sanitized: A recording of this jazz standard begins with Sam Jones playing the bass ostinato "quarter note G, straight eighths B-flat - D, quarter notes E - D" before Miles Davis plays the melody in a rare appearance as sideman. A 1956 film with the same title was inspired by a recording of this standard on Nat King Cole Sings for Two in Love. This standard's A section consists of a circle of fifths progression beginning on the minor four. Although the Real Book renders it in (*) E minor, this standard begins with the notes "G, A, B-flat, long E-flat" in the more common key of G minor, as on Cannonball Adderley's Somethin' Else. Joseph Kosma and Jacques Prevert wrote this originally French-language standard. For 10 points, name this standard about "red and gold" objects that "drift by the window" during the title season.

tossups.question: This is the first word in the title of a “nocturne” composed by Lou Donaldson that appears on his album Blues Walk. The lyrics to a standard whose title begins with this word ask “Why does it seem so inviting?” later noting “It spells the thrill of first-knighting.” A 10-minute cover of a standard whose title begins with this word opens Cannonball Adderley’s album Somethin’ Else. Joseph Kosma and Jacques (*) Prévert co-composed a standard whose title begins with this word; when performed in E minor, that standard opens with the ascending melody E, F-sharp, G, high C. Vernon Duke composed a standard titled for this season “in New York.” For 10 points, name this season whose “leaves” title a jazz standard.
tossups.answer: “Autumn” [accept “Autumn Nocturne” or “Autumn In New York” or “Autumn Leaves”]
tossups.answer_sanitized: "Autumn" [accept "Autumn Nocturne" or "Autumn In New York" or "Autumn Leaves"]
tossups.question_sanitized: This is the first word in the title of a "nocturne" composed by Lou Donaldson that appears on his album Blues Walk. The lyrics to a standard whose title begins with this word ask "Why does it seem so inviting?" later noting "It spells the thrill of first-knighting." A 10-minute cover of a standard whose title begins with this word opens Cannonball Adderley's album Somethin' Else. Joseph Kosma and Jacques (*) Prevert co-composed a standard whose title begins with this word; when performed in E minor, that standard opens with the ascending melody E, F-sharp, G, high C. Vernon Duke composed a standard titled for this season "in New York." For 10 points, name this season whose "leaves" title a jazz standard.

bonuses.leadin: This musician’s trio recorded their signature standard “Poinciana” (“poyn-see-ANN-uh”) on the live album At the Pershing: But Not for Me. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This musician's trio recorded their signature standard "Poinciana" ("poyn-see-ANN-uh") on the live album At the Pershing: But Not for Me. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this jazz pianist who claimed to “honor” the space between notes. This longtime friend of Miles Davis changed his name after converting to Islam.
Jamal’s laid-back playing helped develop this subgenre of jazz that was prominent on the West Coast. This subgenre is named for a concept whose “Birth” titles a Miles Davis album.

Jamal’s 2017 performance of this standard at the Palais des Congrès (“pah-LAY day con-GRAY”) Paris begins with a three-minute-long intro. This standard, which begins Cannonball Adderley’s album Somethin’ Else, is titled for objects that “drift by the window.”
parts_sanitized: Name this jazz pianist who claimed to "honor" the space between notes. This longtime friend of Miles Davis changed his name after converting to Islam.
Jamal's laid-back playing helped develop this subgenre of jazz that was prominent on the West Coast. This subgenre is named for a concept whose "Birth" titles a Miles Davis album.

Jamal's 2017 performance of this standard at the Palais des Congres ("pah-LAY day con-GRAY") Paris begins with a three-minute-long intro. This standard, which begins Cannonball Adderley's album Somethin' Else, is titled for objects that "drift by the window."
answers_sanitized: cool jazz [accept Birth of the Cool]

packet.name: I - McGill B, UCLA A, Michigan B, Georgia Tech C, Southampton B

bonuses.leadin: Examples of this general type of composition include “Ain’t Misbehavin’” and “Stella by Starlight.” For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Examples of this general type of composition include "Ain't Misbehavin'" and "Stella by Starlight." For 10 points each:
parts: Give this term for an often-recorded jazz composition that is an essential part of the repertoire. Many of these pieces are included in the Great American Songbook.

According to Philippe Baudoin, this jazz standard originally composed by Joseph Kosma has been recorded about 1400 times. When performed in its most common key of G minor, this jazz standard begins (read slowly) “G, A, B-flat, long E-flat” over a C minor seventh chord.
Several of this composer’s compositions have gone on to become jazz standards, including “I Got Rhythm” and “Summertime,” the latter of which is from his opera Porgy and Bess.
parts_sanitized: Give this term for an often-recorded jazz composition that is an essential part of the repertoire. Many of these pieces are included in the Great American Songbook.

According to Philippe Baudoin, this jazz standard originally composed by Joseph Kosma has been recorded about 1400 times. When performed in its most common key of G minor, this jazz standard begins (read slowly) "G, A, B-flat, long E-flat" over a C minor seventh chord.
Several of this composer's compositions have gone on to become jazz standards, including "I Got Rhythm" and "Summertime," the latter of which is from his opera Porgy and Bess.

bonuses.leadin: This song’s lyrics were written by Jacques Prevert to music by the Hungarian composer Joseph Kosma, prompting jazz historian Phillipe Baudoin to call it ‘the most important non-American standard’. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this jazz standard. A version of it opens the classic hard bop album Somethin’ Else, which to clarify is written without exclamation marks.
Somethin’ Else is a hard bop album by this saxophonist, who recounts how Joe Zawinul told him the title advice in the spoken intro to his song Mercy, Mercy, Mercy.
The title track of Somethin’ Else was written by this other musician. The year after, Adderley was one of the saxophonists who played on this trumpeter’s album Kind of Blue.
answers: Cannonball Adderley [accept either underlined part; accept Adderley alone, but do NOT accept or prompt on ‘Nat Adderley’]
answers_sanitized: Cannonball Adderley [accept either underlined part; accept Adderley alone, but do NOT accept or prompt on 'Nat Adderley']
